introStatement: I am a collaborative and people-centered leader who values inclusivity, trust, and growth. I am passionate about creating environments where individuals feel heard, supported, and empowered to reach their full potential. Through my background in organizational leadership, caregiving, and mentorship, I bring empathy, clear communication, and a strong commitment to helping others succeed. I believe effective leadership is not just about guiding teams, but about uplifting others and creating meaningful, lasting impact.

education.degree: Bachelor of Applied Science, BAS-OLTM - Organizational Leadership & Technical Management
education.description: Focused on leadership, strategy, collaboration, and inclusive organizational growth.

education.degree: Associate of Applied Science in Medical Administration
education.description: Built strong administrative, communication, and healthcare systems knowledge.

workExperience.description: Serve as a peer mentor to 200+ college students, helping Scholars to navigate academic and professional resources, supporting them in college and WSOS processes, sharing opportunities, and building a sense of community.
Support quadriplegic patients with daily living activities, physical care, emotional well-being, and greater independence in the home environment.
Oversaw day-to-day operations, led a team of approximately 15 employees, managed hiring and training, and improved workflow, service quality, and inventory control.

skills: Ability to Multitask and Manage Multiple Projects

projects.title: Capstone
projects.description:
My Capstone project represents the culmination of my journey through the Organizational Leadership and Technical Management (OLTM) program, integrating three key components: my digital portfolio, leadership challenge, and service project. This comprehensive showcase highlights my transformation from an introverted team member to a confident, inclusive leader who prioritizes empathy, collaboration, and adaptability. The digital portfolio includes this website, a blog outlining my leadership mission statement, and links to professional social media platforms. My leadership challenge draws from "The Leadership Challenge" by James M. Kouzes and Barry Z. Posner (2023), using the textbook and workbook, to practice best leadership practices through weekly in-class discussions and reflections on my role as a Career and Technical Scholar Lead at Washington State Opportunity Scholarship (WSOS), mentoring over 200 college students. My service project involves volunteering with WSOS 3 hours weekly over 8 weeks, with detailed logs of responsibilities and leadership notes, and keeping a weekly journal that summarizes activities and leadership applications, plus weekly journal entries connecting my experiences to course concepts. These elements reflect the profound personal meaning this program holds for me, transforming challenges like overcoming impatience in team settings and building trust across cultural divides into strengths that define my leadership style.

This Capstone project serves as a bridge between my past experiences as a restaurant manager and home care aid, the insights gained through OLTM, and my aspirations to lead organizational initiatives that uplift others. It illustrates how the program has reshaped my perspective, emphasizing inclusive environments where everyone feels valued, and informs my vision for a career in case management and mentorship. Ultimately, this project not only captures my growth but also positions me to contribute authentically to future roles, driven by a commitment to inspiring growth, empowering success, and leading with empathy.

projects.title: COIL Project: Final Group Presentation on COIL & Social Media Influences
projects.description:
The Collaborative Online International Learning (COIL) Project was a five-week virtual collaboration between students from Lower Columbia College (LCC) and the University of Sharjah in the United Arab Emirates. This project focused on exploring cultural differences in digital identity and social media use, allowing us to compare perspectives shaped by distinct cultural and societal norms. Through cross-cultural collaboration, I developed stronger communication skills, particularly in navigating time zones, language nuances, and differing viewpoints. I also strengthened my leadership and teamwork skills by contributing to group discussions and coordinating our final presentation.

This project is especially relevant to my academic and professional development because it enhanced my cultural competence, which is an essential skill in today's globalized and diverse workplace. Understanding how culture influences communication and digital behavior prepares me to work more effectively in team environments, particularly in roles that require collaboration across diverse populations. This experience reinforced the importance of adaptability, empathy, and inclusive communication.

projects.title: Progressive Journal
projects.description:
This Journal is a window into my growth as a leader throughout the Developing and Leading High Performance Teams course. It is an important artifact because it reflects my growth as a collaborative leader. In this journal, I examine my strengths, including being organized, empathetic, and goal-oriented, while also identifying areas where I can continue to improve, such as patience and allowing others more opportunities to lead. 

This reflection is valuable to my capstone because it demonstrates self-awareness, adaptability, and a commitment to continuous growth, all of which are important qualities for effective leadership. It also shows that I value inclusive team environments and understand the importance of creating an environment where all members feel respected and supported. By working in diverse, virtual teams, I learned that successful leadership requires both direction and flexibility. This reflection connects directly to my leadership development because it highlights how I work with others, navigate conflict, and support shared goals. Overall, this artifact represents my ability to reflect on team dynamics and apply leadership concepts in practical, meaningful ways.

projects.title: Leadership Portfolio
projects.description:
My Leadership Portfolio, from the Transformative Leadership and Decision-Making course, is one of the most meaningful artifacts because it presents my personal philosophy of leadership. In this portfolio, I explain that leadership is not about authority or control, but about empowering others, building trust, creating an inclusive environment, and helping people reach their full potential. 

This artifact is especially relevant to my capstone because it reflects the values that guide my leadership: empathy, authenticity, accountability, and adaptability. It also shows how my experiences in mentorship, service, and group projects have shaped my understanding of leadership as an ongoing learning process. One of the most significant lessons from this portfolio is that effective leadership requires reflection and the willingness to grow through experience. This idea connects directly to my capstone because it demonstrates how I strive to support others while continuing to develop my own skills. Overall, this artifact captures both my leadership values and my commitment to helping others succeed.

projects.title: Workforce Communication Assessment
projects.description:
My Workforce Communication Assessment, from the Professional Organizational Communications course, is an important artifact because it demonstrates my ability to evaluate communication practices within an organization and identify opportunities for improvement. In this assessment, I analyzed the communication structure, culture, stakeholder relationships, and challenges within the Cowlitz Youth Soccer Association. 

This artifact is relevant to my capstone because it shows that I understand how communication affects trust, transparency, and organizational effectiveness. It also reflects my interest in inclusive communication and my ability to think critically about how organizations can better serve their communities. Through this project, I learned how that strong communication systems are essential for creating clarity, building relationships, and supporting organizational goals. This connects to my leadership development because it shows that I value communication not only as a skill, but also understand how to use it strategically to support positive change. Overall, this artifact highlights my ability to observe, analyze, and recommend solutions in a real-world organizational setting.

projects.title: Shark Tank
projects.description:
My Shark Tank project, from the Small Group Communication course, is relevant to my capstone because it demonstrates the importance of communication, structure, and teamwork in achieving a common goal. In this project, my group quickly established roles, set communication norms, and worked together to complete the assignment effectively. I served as the coordinator, enabling me to apply my organizational skills to keep the team focused and on track. 

This artifact is significant because it shows my ability to support group success through preparation, clear communication, and flexibility. When our group lost a member, we had to adjust our responsibilities and adapt our plan, which reinforced the value of resilience, trust, and cooperation. For my capstone, this project is meaningful because it reflects my ability to help build a productive and respectful team environment. It also shows that I can contribute to group success by staying organized, communicating clearly, and supporting others through change.
